risk_title: Macroeconomic Crisis Affecting Project Sustainability
risk_summary: Lebanon's severe macroeconomic crisis (rated 'Substantial' in SORT) is creating structural challenges for project implementation and the sustainability of social services for vulnerable groups, particularly given the project's reliance on local NGO partners operating in a deteriorating economic environment.
evidence_quote: Macroeconomic Substantial Substantial Substantial

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints in Fragile Context
risk_summary: Moderate institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability poses risks to the project's ability to deliver services through local NGO sub-grantees in Lebanon's fragile governance environment, with capacity-building efforts still ongoing.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability Moderate Moderate Moderate

risk_title: Fiduciary Risk in Conflict-Affected Setting
risk_summary: Substantial fiduciary risk rating reflects challenges in financial management and accountability mechanisms within Lebanon's fragile institutional context, affecting the project's ability to ensure proper fund utilization across 11 sub-projects.
evidence_quote: Fiduciary Substantial Substantial Substantial

risk_title: Owner Access Denial Blocking Structural Works
risk_summary: Rehabilitation of Re_679 was significantly delayed when an owner on the third floor refused to grant access to their apartment, blocking structural works on the second floor and above. Access was eventually obtained through legal channels, but this demonstrates vulnerability to individual owner obstruction in a fragile post-conflict environment.
evidence_quote: The rehabilitation of Re_679 has been delayed due to the refusal of an owner on the third floor to grant access to their apartment, which in turn has blocked structural works on the second floor and above (the building has eight floors in total). Since the last mission, access to the apartment has been obtained through legal channels, and UN-Habitat has been able to remove the furniture and receive the unit.

risk_title: Accumulated Utility Bills Creating Access Barriers for Rehabilitated Units
risk_summary: Units have accumulated electricity and water bills over five years despite being vacant since the blast. Service providers are reluctant to reconnect utilities, requiring high-level negotiations. This creates barriers to occupancy and beneficiary access to rehabilitated housing in a context of economic fragility.
evidence_quote: In some cases, this required high-level discussions with service providers, as many units have accumulated bills over the past five years, even though they have been vacant since the explosion.

risk_title: Tight Implementation Timeline with No Slippage Tolerance in High-Risk Buildings
risk_summary: Three buildings (Ac_726, Re_679, Re_710) have compressed completion timelines with no room for unforeseen challenges. Re_679 and Re_710 are flagged as high-risk with October completion deadlines. Any disruption—security incidents, labor disputes, or supply chain issues—could prevent completion before project closing on October 31, 2025.
evidence_quote: Progress on the buildings flagged as high-risk is on track, but the timeline for rehabilitation works is tight, with no room for slippages... However, the timeline for completing the works is tight, leaving no room for any unforeseen challenges.

risk_title: Potential Post-Closure Environmental and Social Compliance Gaps
risk_summary: A Post-Closure Action Plan (PCAP) has been prepared as a contingency for worst-case scenarios where activities may not be completed by the closing date (October 31, 2025). This indicates concern that E&S requirements may not be fully met, particularly for Re_679 and Re_710, creating potential compliance and reputational risks.
evidence_quote: The mission discussed potential worst-case scenarios in which certain activities may not be completed by the closing date, thereby triggering specific environmental and social (E&S) requirements—particularly for Re_679 and Re_710. The Bank outlined the circumstances under which a PCAP would be required and the key elements it should contain. A draft PCAP has been prepared by the Bank team and was shared with UN-Habitat as a contingency measure.

risk_title: Incidents and Accidents During Reconstruction Works
risk_summary: Two incidents occurred in August 2025 during rehabilitation works. While addressed appropriately, ongoing construction in a post-blast environment with compressed timelines and extended working hours (including weekends and holidays) creates elevated occupational health and safety risks.
evidence_quote: UN-Habitat informed the Bank team that two incidents occurred in August 2025 and were addressed appropriately. The Bank shared the Environmental and Social Incident Reporting Tool (ESIRT) protocol with UN-Habitat to ensure proper reporting.

risk_title: High Volume of Stakeholder Grievances Related to Reconstruction Activities
risk_summary: 742 stakeholder grievances have been recorded since project start (660 grievances and 182 inquiries), with 122 recorded since May 2025. Most relate to dust, noise, and owner inquiries. This reflects community tensions and dissatisfaction with reconstruction impacts in a post-conflict urban setting.
evidence_quote: Under Component 1, a total of 742 stakeholder grievances have been recorded and addressed since the beginning of the project (660 grievances and 182 inquiries), out of which 122 were recorded since May 2025. Most relate to owners' inquiries and local residents' complaints about dust and noise.

risk_title: Governance Capacity Constraints Affecting Project Closure and Sustainability
risk_summary: Weak institutional capacity and governance fragility are preventing timely operationalization of rehabilitated infrastructure, with the bidding process for facility operator delayed until after project closure, jeopardizing sustainability and beneficiary realization.
evidence_quote: Although the Karantina facility has been rehabilitated, the bidding process to select an operator is expected to commence after the closure of the project.

risk_title: Political Instability Delaying Critical Environmental Remediation Activities
risk_summary: Political and governance fragility is preventing the implementation of environmentally critical contaminated rubble management activities within the project timeline, requiring transfer to a follow-on project pending Parliament ratification.
evidence_quote: However, activities aimed at ensuring the environmentally safe management of contaminated rubble cannot be implemented within the current project timeline. It has been agreed that these activities will be carried out under the World Bank-funded Lebanon Emergency Assistance Project which is pending ratification by Parliament.

risk_title: Delayed Project Effectiveness Affecting Implementation Timeline
risk_summary: The project experienced significant delays in achieving effectiveness (14+ months between signing on 08-May-2024 and effectiveness on 04-Aug-2025), reflecting governance and political obstacles in ratifying loan agreements. This compressed timeline threatens implementation of planned activities.
evidence_quote: The Lebanese Parliament has authorized the Government of Lebanon to ratify the Loan Agreement (Loan No. 9576-LB) by Law No. 17 dated July 11, 2025... the project was declared effective on August 4, 2025.

risk_title: Minimal Project Disbursement Despite Effectiveness
risk_summary: Only 0.25% of project funds ($0.50M of $200M) have been disbursed as of August 2025, despite project effectiveness. This suggests implementation bottlenecks in Lebanon's fragile institutional environment that may prevent timely fund utilization and activity execution.

risk_title: Displacement Pressures Affecting Service Delivery Targets
risk_summary: The project targets displaced Syrians in Lebanon (180,000 beneficiaries planned), but zero beneficiaries have been enrolled to date as of August 2025, indicating implementation challenges in reaching this vulnerable displaced population amid ongoing displacement and conflict-related pressures.

risk_summary: The document acknowledges a 'challenging situation in the country' that could impede project implementation timelines and effectiveness, though specific FCV dynamics are not detailed.
evidence_quote: Despite the challenging situation in the country, preparatory activities are progressing and the Ministry of Environment remains committed to implement the project in a timely manner.

risk_title: High Political and Governance Risk in Lebanon
risk_summary: Lebanon faces sustained high political and governance risk that directly affects project implementation capacity and stability. This risk has persisted across all rating cycles and remains at the highest severity level.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance - Rating at Approval: High, Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 04 - 11 Apr 2024: High

evidence_quote: Risk Category: Macroeconomic - Rating at Approval: High, Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 04 - 11 Apr 2024: High

risk_title: Refugee and Host Community Pressures on Health System
risk_summary: The project explicitly targets refugee populations (Syrian and Palestinian) and host communities, indicating displacement-related pressures on Lebanon's fragile health system. Project indicators track refugee utilization of services, reflecting underlying FCV-related population pressures.
evidence_quote: Percentage of hospital visits by refugees (Syrians and Palestinians) in public hospitals which have benefited from project support (Percentage) - Baseline 35.00 May/2022, Target 40 30-Dec-2025

risk_title: Owner Disputes and Access Restrictions Due to Alleged Theft
risk_summary: Rehabilitation works for building RE_679 have been paused due to an alleged theft incident on June 1-2, 2024, where owners claim valuables and cash worth US$70,000 were stolen from a safe. Owners subsequently restricted site access, demanding compensation, increased transparency, and reduced delays. This has caused at least a 6-week delay in rehabilitation works and creates risk of contract termination if parties cannot reach agreement.
evidence_quote: Over the weekend of June 1-2, 2024, an alleged theft occurred in building Re_679, one of the buildings currently under rehabilitation. The owner claims that a safe, left on site in one of the apartments, was broken into, stealing valuables and cash valuing US$70,000. Subsequently, the owners restricted access to the site, demanding compensation, increased transparency, and reduced delays.

risk_title: Family Disputes Delaying Deed of Commitments Signatures
risk_summary: Family disputes among building owners regarding terms of Deeds of Commitment (DoCs) have significantly delayed the securing of 3 DoCs, which are crucial for commencement of rehabilitation works. Disagreements among family members have stalled progress and made it challenging to obtain necessary consents in a timely manner.
evidence_quote: Family disputes and the time taken by some owners to review the documents have significantly delayed the securing of 3 DoCs, which are crucial for the commencement of rehabilitation works. In several cases, disagreements among family members regarding the terms of the DoC have stalled progress, making it challenging to obtain the necessary consents in a timely manner.

risk_title: Public Sector Capacity Constraints Affecting Permitting Process
risk_summary: Reduction in working hours of the public sector has caused substantial delays in permit approval processes, affecting 2 buildings. These disruptions have delayed the start of rehabilitation works and represent a governance fragility issue affecting project implementation.
evidence_quote: The process of obtaining permits has not been obtained for 2 buildings due to a reduction in working hours of the public sectors. These disruptions have caused substantial delays in the approval process, which in turn has delayed the start of rehabilitation works.

risk_title: Reputational Risk from Building Cancellations and Unmet Owner Expectations
risk_summary: Proposed cancellation of buildings (RE_364, RE_679, and potentially RE_714) creates reputational risks as owner and tenant expectations have been raised through consultation processes. The World Bank notes that reputational and other risks would likely be higher if works were started but not completed prior to project closing date, creating tension between development actors and beneficiary communities.
evidence_quote: UN-Habitat noted that cancellation would result in reputational risks, given the owners and tenant's expectations have been raised through the consultation process to date. The World Bank notes that reputational and other risks would likely be higher in the case that works were started but not completed prior to the project closing date.

risk_title: Tight Project Timeline Creating Risk of Incomplete Works and Financing Ineligibility
risk_summary: The project closing date of June 30, 2025 creates a compressed schedule with little buffer for delays. Multiple buildings have expected completion dates in May-June 2025, leaving minimal time for unforeseen challenges. Expenditures for works carried out after the closing date would not be eligible for World Bank financing, creating pressure on implementation and potential conflict between project requirements and beneficiary needs.
evidence_quote: The project faces considerable implementation risks due to the tight timeline for completing the rehabilitation works, particularly for buildings with expected completion dates in May and June 2025. With the project closing date set for June 30, 2025, this compressed schedule leaves little room for additional delays or unforeseen challenges, which could significantly impact the project's completion within the current project timeframe.

risk_title: Security situation delays project implementation in southern Lebanon
risk_summary: Unstable security conditions in Nabatieh and Tyre service zones are impacting data collection and project activities, requiring shift to virtual meetings and remote tools to mitigate risks.
evidence_quote: Given the unstable security situation in Nabatieh and Tyre, the mission recommended that data collection efforts are based on virtual meetings and remote tools where possible to mitigate security risks and ensure continuity of the work.

risk_title: Component 2 implementation jeopardized by security escalation
risk_summary: Security situation escalation in Lebanon has caused slippage in Component 2 implementation, potentially jeopardizing project development objectives. Activities were previously put on hold due to security concerns.
evidence_quote: Component 2 observed a slippage in implementation due to the security situation, its implementation may be jeopardizing the project development objectives.

risk_title: Stakeholder opposition and threats to Srar Sanitary Landfill project
risk_summary: A landowner's brother operating an adjacent open dump has filed a grievance opposing the Srar SLF and is threatening to damage the facility if not included in the project. This requires resolution before civil works can commence.
evidence_quote: The complainant opposes the SLF because is operates an open dump landfill on the adjacent land plot. He is insisting to be included in the project as well, otherwise he is threatening he will damage the SLF supported by the project.

risk_title: Conflict-related rubble management cannot be completed within project timeline
risk_summary: Management of asbestos-contaminated rubble from the 2023-2024 conflict at the Bakalian site cannot be completed within the Beirut project's timeline, requiring budget cancellation and deferral to future operations.
evidence_quote: The management of asbestos-contaminated rubble at the Bakalian site including its safe disposal cannot be completed within the project's timeline and therefore the budget allocated to this activity will be cancelled.

risk_title: Delayed ESIA for Nabatieh SLF due to security issues
risk_summary: Environmental and Social Impact Assessment for the Nabatieh sanitary landfill was delayed due to security issues, now under development since February 2025 with expected finalization by August 2025.
evidence_quote: The ESIA for the Nabatieh SLF was delayed due to security issues, however it is currently under development since February 2025 and is expected to be finalized by August 2025.

risk_title: Occupational health and safety violations at Karantina facility amid conflict recovery
risk_summary: Multiple serious OHS violations observed at Karantina Sorting Facility including inadequate PPE, unsecured work areas, and unreported incidents requiring hospitalization. Workers' safety at risk during post-conflict reconstruction.
evidence_quote: Workers were not equipped with appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), and none were wearing safety shoes... OHS incidents resulting in emergency hospital visits were recorded in the incident log however they were not reported to the World Bank.

risk_title: Governance fragility affecting fund transfers and project execution
risk_summary: Delays in transferring funds from Ministry of Finance treasury account to project Designated Account, with withdrawal applications facing extensive delays in processing by MoF and MoE, indicating governance and institutional capacity challenges.
evidence_quote: The withdrawal application requested by MoE in October 2024 has faced extensive delays in being processed by the Ministry of Finance (MoF) and MoE and is now being actively followed up by MoE to ensure its timely receipt in the project's designated account.

risk_title: Land compensation issues linked to economic crisis and displacement
risk_summary: 52% of expropriated landowners have not collected compensation due to Lebanese lira depreciation and absentee owners who left Lebanon, creating grievance risks and potential project delays for Zahle sewer network.
evidence_quote: Remaining 13 plots, involving 27 landowners (52 %) still did not collect the compensation, mainly because the owners live abroad, the devaluation of the LBP decreased substantially the compensation amount in real terms, which combined with the complexity of the procedures to claim the compensation under the Lebanese Law left no incentive to collect the compensation.

risk_title: UNDP procurement delays due to internal governance and coordination challenges
risk_summary: Beirut project faces continued delays in procurement due to lengthy internal UNDP procedures and external coordination challenges among government stakeholders, resulting in USD 2.2 million budget cancellation and activities no longer feasible.
evidence_quote: These delays are largely attributed to lengthy internal procedures and approval requirements within UNDP, which have significantly slowed implementation. Additionally, external coordination challenges among key government stakeholders have further compounded these delays.

risk_title: NGO Partner Capacity and Sustainability Challenges from Loss of Core Funding
risk_summary: Several supported NGOs have lost core financing previously sourced from US resources and other development partners, creating near-term capacity and sustainability challenges that strain sub-grant negotiations and partner operations.
evidence_quote: a key impact of the changing financing environment is the loss, for several supported NGOs, of core financing that was previously sourced from US resources or development partners managing these. This has created near-term capacity and sustainability challenges for some.

risk_title: Contraction in International Development Financing Affecting NGO Sector Viability
risk_summary: The international development financing environment is experiencing significant contraction, forcing NGOs to scale back operations and intensifying competition for limited resources, which has translated into difficult sub-grant negotiations and mobilization delays.
evidence_quote: The international development financing environment is currently experiencing significant contraction, and this is having a profound impact on the NGO sector. Many non-governmental organizations are facing severe funding shortages as traditional donors reduce their aid budgets due to global economic challenges and shifting priorities. This financial strain has forced NGOs to scale back their operations, limit their programs, and in some cases, cease their activities altogether.

risk_title: Strain on NGO Partner Relations from Enhanced Due Diligence Processes
risk_summary: Enhanced due diligence processes necessary to minimize fiduciary risk have created periods of strain in relationships with local NGO partners, particularly given the additional burdens imposed by the contracting financing environment.
evidence_quote: Despite these periods, which can strain relationships with local NGO partners, especially given the additional burdens imposed by the contracting financing environment, IRC has continued to manage partnerships effectively.

risk_title: Grievance mechanism gaps for workers at Karantina facility
risk_summary: Workers report grievances verbally on site and these are included in contractor reports, but UNDP does not record them in the grievance log or reports to the Bank, creating accountability gaps.
evidence_quote: During the site visit, it was noted that workers report grievance, feedback or complaints on site, often verbally and these are included in the Contractor's monthly reports, however UNDP does not record these in the grievance log and in the reports to the Bank.

risk_title: Land expropriation compensation delays affecting project beneficiaries
risk_summary: 52% of expropriated landowners (27 out of 50) have not collected compensation for land taken for Zahle sewer network, primarily due to absentee owners who left Lebanon and currency depreciation making compensation negligible.
evidence_quote: Remaining 13 plots, involving 27 landowners (52 %) still did not collect the compensation, mainly because the owners live abroad, the devaluation of the LBP decreased substantially the compensation amount in real terms, which combined with the complexity of the procedures to claim the compensation under the Lebanese Law left no incentive to collect the compensation.

risk_title: Conflict-driven economic crisis reducing landowner incentive to claim compensation
risk_summary: The 2019 economic crisis and Lebanese lira depreciation have reduced compensation amounts to negligible levels (USD 27-728 per plot), eliminating incentive for landowners to claim expropriation compensation.

risk_title: Delayed Project Effectiveness Due to Parliamentary Ratification Requirements
risk_summary: The IBRD loan Financing Agreement is undergoing government internal clearances before parliamentary ratification, a country requirement for loan effectiveness. This delays project commencement during the critical post-war recovery phase.
evidence_quote: The Financing Agreement (IBRD loan) is now undergoing government internal clearances before being submitted to the Parliament for ratification, a country requirement for loan effectiveness. As the project aims to help Lebanon restore core fiscal management functions, namely domestic revenue mobilization and the accountable allocation and use of public resources, timely commencement of the project is even more critical for the post-war recovery phase.
